JSParser --> |Returns graph| FastAPI
FastAPI --> |Call OpenRouter API| LLM[ai_service.py]
LLM --> |JSON Insights| FastAPI
FastAPI --> |VisualizeResponse| VSCode
VSCode --> Webview[Render SVG Diagrams in Webview Panel]

end

sequence:
sequenceDiagram
actor Dev
Dev->>Ext: Selects code & Run command
Ext->>Server: POST /visualize (code)
Server->>JS Parser: Extract abstract syntax tree

Server->>AI model: analyze_code(code) via OpenRouter

classDiagram

call_graph:
graph LR
activate --> registerCommand_visualizeFile
registerCommand_visualizeFile --> VisualizerPanel.createOrShow
registerCommand_visualizeFile --> fetch_visualize_API
fetch_visualize_API --> visualize_endpoint
visualize_endpoint --> _run_ast_pipeline
visualize_endpoint --> analyze_code
_run_ast_pipeline --> subprocess_run_node
explanation: This codebase represents a Visual Studio Code extension accompanied by a Python backend for intelligent code visualization. The entry point at the frontend allows users to select text or multiple workspace files, prompting the extension to bundle and send this source code over to the robust FastAPI-driven backend. At its core, the backend server pipelines the code sequentially: first offloading deterministic AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) generation to a Node.js process (`runParser.js`), and concurrently consulting a semantic AI model (via OpenRouter integration in `ai_service.py`) for deeper insights, flowchart semantics, and system architectural views natively in Mermaid.js syntax. Finally, the synthesis of this data is sent back across HTTP directly wrapping into a dynamic local Webview panel initialized by the frontend, offering an interactive visual playground of the repository.
workflow: User triggers 'aiCodeVisualizer.visualizeFile' or 'analyzeWorkspace' inside VS Code.
Extension fetches the active document or relevant matched Python assets and issues an HTTP POST containing the file data to the `http://localhost:8000/visualize` Python server.
FastAPI receives the payload, spins up `_run_ast_pipeline` which calls out to the underlying local AST module `runParser.js` mapped locally.
The code is forwarded immediately parallel-to standard processing to `ai_service.py` to extract conceptual complexity, dependencies, and complex topological Mermaid diagram code.
The API constructs a unified `VisualizeResponse` dictionary and returns it back out over CORS-enabled routes.

The frontend `VisualizerPanel` captures this response using the Fetch API, injects it into a curated HTML string dynamically utilizing the Mermaid.js standard library, and completes DOM updates showing flowcharts, class graphs, explanations, and architectural sequences natively inside the editor.
summary: An AI-powered local Visual Studio Code Extension and external FastAPI server system that automatically visualizes developer code into rich diagrams and structural explanations using Mermaid.js syntax mapping and node graph abstraction extraction.
